query: Port of Baltimore Carnival Cruise Line contract extension 2025 year-round service through 2030
a_issues: One irrelevant result is included about JaxPort/Carnival in Jacksonville, which could distract a model. One result is from 2026 and mentions the Carnival Pride operating under the new five-year contract, but it is consistent with the core facts. Otherwise the key facts are accurate: new five-year agreement, effective January 1, 2025, with a five-year renewal option, and year-round service from Baltimore.
b_issues: Results are mostly secondary reports and snippets rather than the primary Maryland government source. The snippets confirm a five-year agreement effective January 1, 2025 with a five-year renewal option, but they provide less direct detail about year-round service and do not as clearly ground the answer in the official announcement. No major factual contradiction is visible in the snippets.
reasoning: Content A better enables a correct answer because it includes the official Governor of Maryland press release stating the decisive facts: Carnival will continue serving the Port of Baltimore for another five years, the agreement takes effect January 1, 2025, and it includes a five-year renewal option. A also contains supporting coverage explicitly noting Carnival Pride’s year-round Baltimore service. That is enough for an LLM to answer that the contract runs from 2025 through 2029, with an option that could extend service through 2030 and beyond depending on exercise of the renewal. Content B has largely accurate snippets, but they are all secondary and less detailed, with no primary-source result and weaker support for the year-round-service part of the query.
